mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy path is clearly downward.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips and Rank IC stays below 0.03 in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above 5% signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% and rises at least 0.2pp over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below unconditional baseline by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous pickup in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand acceleration across consumption and housing, which supports earnings momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or if positive returns do not occur more often when both series are rising than when only one is rising.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO relative to prior months indicates improving industrial output and capacity utilization, which supports cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of months after two consecutive INDPRO increases, or if the effect disappears in a rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth followed by firmer PAYEMS indicates synchronized output and payroll expansion, which improves earnings visibility and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-payroll signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered instances do not deliver positive 20-day returns at least 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still below inflation, markets expect policy tightening and margin pressure, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if the spread condition is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the average forward return is not materially below baseline during spread-expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS after prior hiring strength signals labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in a majority of PAYEMS rollover episodes, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for UNRATE changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led slowdown, falling HOUST together with weak RSAFS identifies demand compression that precedes equity drawdowns, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% over 3 months and RSAFS growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-sales weakness condition is not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the joint signal fails to beat either component alone.
